meta.note: written from preseason composite projections, schedule-aware records, and pick-adjusted strength

2.head: proj 9.4-4.6 · #1 now · S 7.01/5.69/4.95 · avg starter age 26.4
2.text: Best team in the league in all three projected years — and shrinking every one of them. Bijan, Chase, and CMC all decay while the pick cupboard holds exactly one 2028 Late 1st, which means there is no plan B. This roster was built to win a title in 2026-27 and anything else is failure: a second-hardest-in-the-league schedule and a first-round playoff exit would turn this from a dynasty into an estate sale. Every trade this team makes should be judged against one question — does it raise the odds of a ring in the next two Januaries? If the answer is no, it's clutter.

8.head: proj 9.4-4.6 · #2 now · S 6.90/5.69/3.63 · avg starter age 25.5
8.text: Co-favorite on paper, cornered in reality: zero future 1sts, zero 2nds, and the steepest 2028 cliff of any contender — the team is literally named after its biggest faller (Chase Brown, −0.60 by 2028). Daniels and Bowers keep the lights on, but there is no reload coming and no capital to buy one. This is the most all-in roster in the league whether its manager admits it or not. Win now, because 2028 is already spoken for, and it said no.

6.head: proj 8.4-5.6 · #3 now · S 5.98/4.30/4.31 · avg starter age 25.1
6.text: The best-run contender: Nacua in his prime, Achane and Jeanty young and flat instead of declining, and Dak's successor (McCarthy) already rostered before anyone had to panic. Year-3 strength holds where Cops' collapses. The only criticism worth writing is that this team has no excuse to overpay at the deadline — its window doesn't close, so it should let the aging teams above it bid against each other and quietly collect the difference.

1.head: proj 8.2-5.8 · #4 now · S 5.85/4.54/4.84 · avg starter age 24.7
1.text: Youngest contender, only contender whose strength rises again in 2028, and holding a 2027 Mid 1st, a 2028 Early 1st, and three 2nds on top of it. Maye, McBride, Purdy, London — all 25 or under. This is the best long-term position in the league, and the only way to ruin it is impatience. The 8-win projection undersells the leverage: this team can outlast everyone above it without making a single move.

9.head: proj 7.4-6.6 · #5 now · S 5.14/3.39/3.52 · avg starter age 26.6
9.text: Classic no-man's-land, and drifting deeper into it. Gibbs and Herbert are real, but this is the oldest core in the top half — Stafford is 37, the year-2 dropoff is the sharpest among playoff teams, and 'a 1st in each of the next two drafts' is a diversified portfolio, not a direction. Teams that refuse to pick a lane end up 7-7 forever, which is exactly what the model projects. Weeks 6-8 should force the decision this front office has been avoiding.

10.head: proj 7.4-6.6 · #6 now · S 4.78/2.75/1.45 · avg starter age 25.2
10.text: Second-worst 2028 outlook in the league, hidden behind a respectable 2026 record. Two good young QBs is a luxury in superflex — and this roster is spending the luxury on treading water while Henry depreciates and the rest of the depth chart evaporates (S drops 70% by 2028). One of Williams or Nix could fund an entire retool at peak value, today. Standing pat isn't the safe option here; it's the slow version of losing both.

11.head: proj 7.1-6.9 · #7 now · S 4.39/3.17/2.17 · avg starter age 26.3
11.text: The mediocrity treadmill, personified. Hurts and JSN are a real core; Taylor and McLaurin are melting ice cubes; the schedule is the hardest in the middle tier; and the projected outcome — 7.1 wins, the 6-seed or just outside it — is the worst place in a dynasty league to live. Nothing about this roster says title and nothing about it says rebuild. Pick one by the deadline, because the standings are going to pick for you and they have worse taste.

12.head: proj 7.1-6.9 · #9 now · S 4.05/2.41/2.29 · avg starter age 24.4
12.text: The league's easiest schedule is doing a lot of cosmetic work here — 7 projected wins on the 9th-best roster. Young does not mean ascending: outside of Dart (legit, 22), this roster's production is front-loaded anyway, with Cook on the wrong side of the RB curve and Fannin's rookie flash projected to regress to nothing. One Early 2027 1st and two 2nds is seed capital, not a war chest. The honest move is to admit Dart's timeline IS the timeline and trade everything older than he is while it still returns value.

3.head: proj 6.6-7.4 · #8 now · S 4.08/1.94/1.51 · avg starter age 25.0
3.text: This is a Mahomes deposit account with eleven other roster spots attached. He's the only starter above 0.7 WAR, the only one who improves by 2028, and the record will flatter this team just long enough to miss the sell window. Two future 1sts and four 2nds are already banked — the rebuild is half-funded and half-denied. A 29-year-old elite QB is the most valuable chip that exists in superflex, and his price only goes down from here. Cash him or commit to him; the current plan is neither.

7.head: proj 5.8-8.2 · #10 now · S 2.88/1.84/1.02 · avg starter age 26.4
7.text: The most mismanaged asset sheet in the league: Allen and Burrow — two top-8 superflex QBs, a couple full WAR of pure gold — welded to the worst 2028 outlook anyone has, one future 1st, and a week-7 Bills bye that vaporizes 3.5 lineup WAR in a single week. Every contender in the league would pay a fortune for either QB, and this roster converts them into a 6-win season instead. There is no version of holding that works. Sell both, take the haul, and this becomes the league's scariest rebuild by August.

4.head: proj 4.8-9.2 · #11 now · S 2.15/2.70/3.09 · avg starter age 25.0
4.text: The plan is working — one of only two rosters whose strength rises every projected year, with four future 1sts (two Early) behind it. The remaining homework is unsentimental: Lawrence and A.J. Brown are the last veterans worth real capital, and their value decays with every week they stay. Finish the sell-off. The only mistake still available to this team is deciding it's 'close' a year early and spending the war chest on a 6th seed.

5.head: proj 2.4-11.6 · #12 now · S 0.20/2.14/3.16 · avg starter age 23.6
5.text: A 2026 lineup barely above replacement — projected to be one of the worst teams this league has ever fielded — and it doesn't matter at all. Eight future 1sts, four of them Early, the youngest starters in the league, and Love plus Mendoza already rising. The model prices his year-3 WAR at 2.5× year-1, and it's right: 2026 is a formality to be endured, and the 2027 draft, where five of those 1sts convey, is the real opener. The only way to lose from here is to get bored. Don't get bored.
